prompt_summary: Translate each Lean declaration from the Lean statement alone, with no paper context.
Preserve every visible variable, binder, hypothesis, domain condition, equivalence or implication direction, and conclusion.
Do not replace formulas with theorem labels, informal endpoints, or proof-route summaries.
comment: Refreshed Theorem 3 drafts with explicit W/W-prime qualifiers after report-parser cleanup; other drafts preserved.

itemFairness.statement: {m n : ℕ} [NeZero n] (W : RecommendationModel m n) (ρ : Policy m n) = EconCSLib.finiteMin (normalizedItemUtility W ρ)

itemNormalizer.statement: {m n : ℕ} (W : RecommendationModel m n) (j : Item n) = \sum u, W.utility u j

normalizedItemUtility.statement: {m n : ℕ} (W : RecommendationModel m n) (ρ : Policy m n) (j : Item n) = let denom = itemNormalizer W j if denom = 0 then 0 else rawItemUtility W ρ j / denom

normalizedUserUtility.statement: {m n : ℕ} [NeZero n] (W : RecommendationModel m n) (ρ : Policy m n) (u : User m) = rawUserUtility W ρ u / RecommendationModel.bestItemUtility W u

priceOfFairness.statement: {m n : ℕ} [NeZero m] [NeZero n] (W : RecommendationModel m n) = priceOfFairnessAt W 1

priceOfFairnessAt.statement: {m n : ℕ} [NeZero m] [NeZero n] (W : RecommendationModel m n) (\gamma : ℝ) = let base = RecommendationModel.optimalUserFairnessAtLevel W 0 if base = 0 then 0 else (base - RecommendationModel.optimalUserFairnessAtLevel W \gamma) / base

priceOfMisestimation.statement: {m n : ℕ} [NeZero m] [NeZero n] (E : EstimatedRecommendationModel m n) (\gamma : ℝ) (ρhat : Policy m n) = let base = RecommendationModel.optimalUserFairnessAtLevel E.trueModel \gamma if base = 0 then 0 else (base - RecommendationModel.userFairness E.trueModel ρhat) / base

proposition1_symmetric_lp_reduction.statement: \forall {m n K : ℕ} [inst : NeZero m] [inst_1 : NeZero n] [NeZero K] (R : ReductionWitness m n K) (reps : TypeRepresentatives types) (\gamma : ℝ), LPReductionTarget R \gamma

proposition2_symmetric_optimum_exists.statement: \forall {m n K : ℕ} [inst : NeZero m] [inst_1 : NeZero n] [NeZero K] (S : SymmetricData m n K) (reps : TypeRepresentatives types), Positive \to \exists ρsym, IsTypeSymmetric types ρsym \land IsOptimalAtLevel 1 ρsym

rawItemUtility.statement: {m n : ℕ} (W : RecommendationModel m n) (ρ : Policy m n) (j : Item n) = \sum u, W.utility u j * (ρ u j).toReal

rawUserUtility.statement: {m n : ℕ} (W : RecommendationModel m n) (ρ : Policy m n) (u : User m) = EconCSLib.Policy.agentScore ρ W.utility u

recommendationUtility.statement: {m n : ℕ} (W : RecommendationModel m n) (u : User m) (j : Item n) = W.utility u j

solvesProblemOne.statement: {m n : ℕ} [NeZero m] [NeZero n] (W : RecommendationModel m n) (\gamma : ℝ) (ρ : Policy m n) = RecommendationModel.IsOptimalAtLevel W \gamma ρ

theorem3_price_of_fairness_decreases.statement: For recommendation models W and W' with nonnegative utilities and a positive item in every row, if W.optimalUserFairnessAtLevel 1 <= W'.optimalUserFairnessAtLevel 1, then W'.priceOfFairness <= W.priceOfFairness.

theorem3_price_of_fairness_strictly_decr.statement: For recommendation models W and W' with nonnegative utilities and a positive item in every row, if W.optimalUserFairnessAtLevel 1 < W'.optimalUserFairnessAtLevel 1, then W'.priceOfFairness < W.priceOfFairness.

theorem3_price_decreases_first_half.statement: For the opposing two-type source model with positive strictly decreasing item values and 0 < alpha <= alpha' <= 1/2, increasing alpha toward the balanced population weakly decreases the price of fairness.

theorem3_price_increases_second_half.statement: For the opposing two-type source model with positive strictly decreasing item values and 1/2 <= alpha <= alpha' < 1, increasing alpha away from the balanced population weakly increases the price of fairness.

theorem4_misestimation_tradeoff_typeOne.statement: \forall {m n : ℕ} [inst : NeZero m] [inst_1 : NeZero n] (E : EstimatedRecommendationModel m n) (Rtrue : ReductionWitness m n 2) (Rest : ReductionWitness m n 3) (repsTrue : TypeRepresentatives types) (repsEst : TypeRepresentatives types) {beta eps : ℝ} (u : User m), 2 < n \to trueModel = model \to estimatedModel = model \to reduced = twoTypeReducedModel (1 / 2) (theorem4SmallValueVector eps) \to reduced = theorem4EstimatedReducedModel beta (theorem4SmallValueVector eps) \to (\forall (u : User m), toType u = 0 \to toType u = 0) \to (\forall (u : User m), toType u = 1 \to toType u = 1) \to toType u = 1 \to toType u = 2 \to 0 < eps \to (↑n)⁻¹ < beta \to beta < 1 / 2 \to (have ρ0 := theorem4NoFairnessPolicyCollapsed (theorem4SmallValueVector eps); SolvesEstimatedProblem 0 (liftedPolicy ρ0) \land priceOfMisestimation 0 (liftedPolicy ρ0) \le 1 / 2) \land \exists ρ1, SolvesEstimatedProblem 1 (liftedPolicy ρ1) \land 1 - eps < priceOfMisestimation 1 (liftedPolicy ρ1)

theorem4_misestimation_tradeoff_typeZero.statement: \forall {m n : ℕ} [inst : NeZero m] [inst_1 : NeZero n] (E : EstimatedRecommendationModel m n) (Rtrue : ReductionWitness m n 2) (Rest : ReductionWitness m n 3) (repsTrue : TypeRepresentatives types) (repsEst : TypeRepresentatives types) {beta eps : ℝ} (u : User m), 2 < n \to trueModel = model \to estimatedModel = model \to reduced = twoTypeReducedModel (1 / 2) (theorem4SmallValueVector eps) \to reduced = theorem4EstimatedReducedModel beta (theorem4SmallValueVector eps) \to (\forall (u : User m), toType u = 0 \to toType u = 0) \to (\forall (u : User m), toType u = 1 \to toType u = 1) \to toType u = 0 \to toType u = 2 \to 0 < eps \to (↑n)⁻¹ < beta \to beta < 1 / 2 \to (have ρ0 := theorem4NoFairnessPolicyCollapsed (theorem4SmallValueVector eps); SolvesEstimatedProblem 0 (liftedPolicy ρ0) \land priceOfMisestimation 0 (liftedPolicy ρ0) \le 1 / 2) \land \exists ρ1, SolvesEstimatedProblem 1 (liftedPolicy ρ1) \land 1 - eps < priceOfMisestimation 1 (liftedPolicy ρ1)

assumption_positive_recommendation_utili.statement: Source condition: recommendation utilities in the relevant source models are strictly positive.

assumption_theorem3_opposing_type_model.statement: Source condition: Theorem 3 uses the opposing two-type model with the same positive, strictly decreasing value vector.

assumption_theorem3_first_half_alpha_dom.statement: Source condition: Theorem 3 first-half monotonicity uses 0 < alpha <= alpha' <= 1/2.

assumption_theorem3_second_half_alpha_do.statement: Source condition: Theorem 3 second-half monotonicity uses 1/2 <= alpha <= alpha' < 1.

assumption_theorem4_at_least_three_items.statement: Source condition: Theorem 4 uses a nontrivial item geometry with more than two items.

assumption_theorem4_true_model_reduction.statement: Source condition: Theorem 4's true model is the two-type source construction.

assumption_theorem4_estimated_model_redu.statement: Source condition: Theorem 4's estimated model is the three-type estimated construction.

assumption_theorem4_displayed_reduced_mo.statement: Source condition: Theorem 4 uses the displayed true and estimated reduced matrices.

assumption_theorem4_cold_start_type_wiri.statement: Source condition: Theorem 4's cold-start user is estimated as the third type and has one of the two true opposing rows.

assumption_theorem4_parameter_domain.statement: Source condition: Theorem 4 uses positive epsilon and beta in the displayed domain.

userFairness.statement: {m n : ℕ} [NeZero m] [NeZero n] (W : RecommendationModel m n) (ρ : Policy m n) = EconCSLib.finiteMin (normalizedUserUtility W ρ)
